content == {{神社 | 名称=Kiburi Shrine | 画像=[[Image:Kiburi Jinjya01.jpg|250px]]
Torii and haiden | 所在地={{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277}}{{ill|lt=Ibi District|Q1204309|WD=Q1204309}}{{ill|lt=Ōno|Q1203308|WD=Q1203308}}Inatomi Aza Hakusan 2586 | 位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | 祭神={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Izanagi-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q813858}}、{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Izanami-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q682306}}、{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kikuri-hime-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}、{{ill|Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q276944}}、Hakusan Hosaō | 創建=Traditionally {{ill|lt=Jinki|Q1373210|WD=Q1373210}} 2 ({{ill|lt=725|Q23552|WD=Q23552}}) | 社格={{ill|lt=small Shikinaisha|Q134917287|WD=Q134917287}}, former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}} | 例祭=Grand annual festival (first Sunday of April) | 本殿= | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Gifu|label=Kiburi Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Kiburi Shrine''' (きぶりじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in {{ill|lt=Ōno|Q1203308|WD=Q1203308}}, {{ill|lt=Ibi District|Q1204309|WD=Q1204309}}, {{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277}}. It is a {{ill|lt=Shikinaisha|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Shikinaisha}} ({{ill|lt=Mino Province|Q864156|WD=Q864156|lt=Mino no Kuni}} {{ill|lt=Ōno District (Mino)|Q11439757|WD=Q11439757|lt=Ōno District}} Kiburi Shrine, Shōnii ranked Shikinaisha Kiburi Ōkami), and its former rank under the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534}} was {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}}. ===Enshrined deities=== *{{ill|lt=Izanagi|Q813858|WD=Q813858|lt=Izanagi-no-Mikoto}} *{{ill|lt=Izanami|Q682306|WD=Q682306|lt=Izanami-no-Mikoto}} *{{ill|lt=Kikurihime-no-Kami|Q11619891|WD=Q11619891|lt=Kikuri-hime-no-Mikoto}} *{{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto}} *Hakusan Hosaō ===History=== According to shrine tradition, it was founded in {{ill|lt=725|Q23552|WD=Q23552}} ({{ill|lt=Jinki|Q1373210|WD=Q1373210}} 2). On lunar June 18 of Jinki 2, golden-colored snow reportedly fell on the summit of Mt. Hakusan in Ōno District of Mino Province. This phenomenon was interpreted by oracle as the descent of {{ill|lt=Kikurihime-no-Kami|Q11619891|WD=Q11619891|lt=Hakusan-hime-no-Kami}}, and a shrine was built and worshiped on Mt. Hakusan summit. At this time, the nearby {{ill|lt=Hossō|Q349849|WD=Q349849}} temple Shinpuku-ji was renamed {{ill|lt=Kiburi-ji|Q11523695|WD=Q11523695}}. In {{ill|lt=1530|Q6429|WD=Q6429}} ({{ill|lt=Kyōroku|Q1193418|WD=Q1193418}} 3), floods damaged buildings at the foot, and in {{ill|lt=1560|Q6613|WD=Q6613}} ({{ill|lt=Eiroku|Q1143341|WD=Q1143341}} 3), it was burned by {{ill|lt=Oda Nobunaga|Q171411|WD=Q171411}}, and the temple complex was lost. It was later restored with aid such as {{ill|lt=vermilion-seal letters|Q11093673|WD=Q11093673}} issued by {{ill|lt=Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Q187550|WD=Q187550}} and {{ill|lt=Tokugawa Ieyasu|Q171977|WD=Q171977}}, and in {{ill|lt=1677|Q7631|WD=Q7631}} ({{ill|lt=Enpō|Q1145735|WD=Q1145735}} 5) new shrine buildings were constructed with support of the Toda clan of {{ill|lt=Ōgaki Domain|Q839430|WD=Q839430}}. Thereafter the Toda clan of Ōgaki Domain protected the shrine through repairs. It had long stood on the summit of Hakusan (317 m), but because Hakusan area had high-quality {{ill|lt=limestone|Q23757|WD=Q23757}}, {{ill|lt=lime|Q250423|WD=Q250423}} mining began around the area from the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Meiji period|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q3125096}}. In {{ill|lt=1968|Q2644|WD=Q2644}} (Shōwa 43), Hakusan was to be cut for mining, and Kiburi Shrine moved to its current site at the foot of the mountain. In {{ill|lt=1999|Q2091|WD=Q2091}} (Heisei 11), surrounding areas were developed as "Kiburi Fureai Park." === Cultural properties === *Town-designated cultural properties **Chōzu stone (Important Cultural Property, craft){{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://heritage-ono.jp/shiteibunkazai.html |title=List of designated cultural properties |publisher=Ōno Town |accessdate=2013-04-20 |archivedate=2013-05-01 |archiveurl=https://archive.fo/tbePM}} **Kiburi-bayashi (intangible folk cultural property) **Fusulina fossils (natural monument) === Location === *Inatomi Aza Hakusan 2586, Ōno-chō, Ibi District, Gifu Prefecture === Transportation === *About 3.5 km from {{ill|lt=Motosu Station|Q6918526|WD=Q6918526}} on the {{ill|lt=Tarumi Railway|Q11543718|WD=Q11543718}}. *About 3.5 km from {{ill|lt=Kochibora Station|Q6424878|WD=Q6424878}} on the {{ill|lt=Tarumi Railway|Q11543718|WD=Q11543718}}. *About 5 km from {{ill|lt=Ōno Bus Center|Q11439494|WD=Q11439494}} via {{ill|lt=Meihan Kintetsu Bus|Q11415818|WD=Q11415818}} / {{ill|lt=Gifu Bus|Q5560101|WD=Q5560101|lt=Gifu Bus}}. === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === External links === {{commonscat-inline|Kiburi-jinja}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:きふりしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kiburi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kiburi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mino Province]] [[Category:岐阜県の神社]] [[Category:大野町の歴史]] [[Category:美濃国の式内社]] [[Category:岐阜県の旧郷社]] [[Category:岐阜県内の市町村指定有形文化財]] [[Category:区市町村指定無形民俗文化財]] [[Category:区市町村指定天然記念物]] [[Category:大垣藩]] [[Category:大野町の建築物]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:qqq]]

On lunar June 18 of Jinki 2, golden-colored snow reportedly fell on the summit of Mt. Hakusan in Ōno District of Mino Province. This phenomenon was interpreted by oracle as the descent of {{ill|lt=Kikurihime-no-Kami|Q11619891|WD=Q11619891|lt=Hakusan-hime-no-Kami|1=Kukurihime}}, and a shrine was built and worshiped on Mt. Hakusan summit. At this time, the nearby {{ill|lt=Hossō|Q349849|WD=Q349849|1=East Asian Yogācāra}} temple Shinpuku-ji was renamed {{ill|lt=Kiburi-ji|Q11523695|WD=Q11523695|1=Kiburi-ji}}. In {{ill|lt=1530|Q6429|WD=Q6429|1=1530}} ({{ill|lt=Kyōroku|Q1193418|WD=Q1193418|1=Kyōroku}} 3), floods damaged buildings at the foot, and in {{ill|lt=1560|Q6613|WD=Q6613|1=1560}} ({{ill|lt=Eiroku|Q1143341|WD=Q1143341|1=Eiroku}} 3), it was burned by {{ill|lt=Oda Nobunaga|Q171411|WD=Q171411|1=Oda Nobunaga}}, and the temple complex was lost. It was later restored with aid such as {{ill|lt=vermilion-seal letters|Q11093673|WD=Q11093673|1=shuinjō}} issued by {{ill|lt=Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Q187550|WD=Q187550|1=Toyotomi Hideyoshi}} and {{ill|lt=Tokugawa Ieyasu|Q171977|WD=Q171977|1=Tokugawa Ieyasu}}, and in {{ill|lt=1677|Q7631|WD=Q7631|1=1677}} ({{ill|lt=Enpō|Q1145735|WD=Q1145735|1=Enpō}} 5) new shrine buildings were constructed with support of the Toda clan of {{ill|lt=Ōgaki Domain|Q839430|WD=Q839430|1=Ōgaki Domain}}. Thereafter the Toda clan of Ōgaki Domain protected the shrine through repairs. It had long stood on the summit of Hakusan (317 m), but because Hakusan area had high-quality {{ill|lt=limestone|Q23757|WD=Q23757|1=Limestone}}, {{ill|lt=lime|Q250423|WD=Q250423|1=Lime (material)}} mining began around the area from the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Meiji period|1=Meiji era|WD=Q3125096}}. In {{ill|lt=1968|Q2644|WD=Q2644|1=1968}} (Shōwa 43), Hakusan was to be cut for mining, and Kiburi Shrine moved to its current site at the foot of the mountain. In {{ill|lt=1999|Q2091|WD=Q2091|1=1999}} (Heisei 11), surrounding areas were developed as "Kiburi Fureai Park." === Cultural properties === *Town-designated cultural properties **Chōzu stone (Important Cultural Property, craft){{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://heritage-ono.jp/shiteibunkazai.html |title=List of designated cultural properties |publisher=Ōno Town |accessdate=2013-04-20 |archivedate=2013-05-01 |archiveurl=https://archive.fo/tbePM}} **Kiburi-bayashi (intangible folk cultural property) **Fusulina fossils (natural monument) === Location === *Inatomi Aza Hakusan 2586, Ōno-chō, Ibi District, Gifu Prefecture === Transportation === *About 3.5 km from {{ill|lt=Motosu Station|Q6918526|WD=Q6918526|1=Motosu Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Tarumi Railway|Q11543718|WD=Q11543718|1=Tarumi Railway}}. *About 3.5 km from {{ill|lt=Kochibora Station|Q6424878|WD=Q6424878|1=Kochibora Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Tarumi Railway|Q11543718|WD=Q11543718|1=Tarumi Railway}}. *About 5 km from {{ill|lt=Ōno Bus Center|Q11439494|WD=Q11439494|1=Ōno Bus Center}} via {{ill|lt=Meihan Kintetsu Bus|Q11415818|WD=Q11415818|1=Meihan Kintetsu Bus}} / {{ill|lt=Gifu Bus|Q5560101|WD=Q5560101|lt=Gifu Bus|1=Gifu Bus}}. === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === External links === {{commonscat-inline|Kiburi-jinja}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:きふりしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kiburi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kiburi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mino Province]] [[Category:岐阜県の神社]] [[Category:大野町の歴史]] [[Category:美濃国の式内社]] [[Category:岐阜県の旧郷社]] [[Category:岐阜県内の市町村指定有形文化財]] [[Category:区市町村指定無形民俗文化財]] [[Category:区市町村指定天然記念物]] [[Category:大垣藩]] [[Category:大野町の建築物]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:qqq]]

link|Q11523697}} == Japanese content == {{神社 | 名称=Kiburi Shrine | 画像=[[Image:Kiburi Jinjya01.jpg|250px]]
Torii and haiden | 所在地={{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277|1=Gifu Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Ibi District|Q1204309|WD=Q1204309|1=Ibi District, Gifu}}{{ill|lt=Ōno|Q1203308|WD=Q1203308|1=Ōno, Gifu}}Inatomi Aza Hakusan 2586 | 位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | 祭神={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Izanagi-no-Mikoto|1=Izanagi|WD=Q813858}}、{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Izanami-no-Mikoto|1=Izanami|WD=Q682306}}、{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kikuri-hime-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}、{{ill|Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q276944|1=Ōkuninushi}}、Hakusan Hosaō | 創建=Traditionally {{ill|lt=Jinki|Q1373210|WD=Q1373210|1=Jinki (era)}} 2 ({{ill|lt=725|Q23552|WD=Q23552|1=725}}) | 社格={{ill|lt=small Shikinaisha|Q134917287|WD=Q134917287|1=Shikinai Shosha}}, former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} | 例祭=Grand annual festival (first Sunday of April) | 本殿= | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Gifu|label=Kiburi Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Kiburi Shrine''' (きぶりじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Ōno|Q1203308|WD=Q1203308|1=Ōno, Gifu}}, {{ill|lt=Ibi District|Q1204309|WD=Q1204309|1=Ibi District, Gifu}}, {{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277|1=Gifu Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|lt=Shikinaisha|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} ({{ill|lt=Mino Province|Q864156|WD=Q864156|lt=Mino no Kuni|1=Mino Province}} {{ill|lt=Ōno District (Mino)|Q11439757|WD=Q11439757|lt=Ōno District|1=Ōno district}} Kiburi Shrine, Shōnii ranked Shikinaisha Kiburi Ōkami), and its former rank under the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. ===Enshrined deities=== *{{ill|lt=Izanagi|Q813858|WD=Q813858|lt=Izanagi-no-Mikoto|1=Izanagi}} *{{ill|lt=Izanami|Q682306|WD=Q682306|lt=Izanami-no-Mikoto|1=Izanami}} *{{ill|lt=Kikurihime-no-Kami|Q11619891|WD=Q11619891|lt=Kikuri-hime-no-Mikoto|1=Kukurihime}} *{{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōkuninushi}} *Hakusan Hosaō ===History=== According to shrine tradition, it was founded in {{ill|lt=725|Q23552|WD=Q23552|1=725}} ({{ill|lt=Jinki|Q1373210|WD=Q1373210|1=Jinki (era)}} 2). On lunar June 18 of Jinki 2, golden-colored snow reportedly fell on the summit of Mt. Hakusan in Ōno District of Mino Province. This phenomenon was interpreted by oracle as the descent of {{ill|lt=Kikurihime-no-Kami|Q11619891|WD=Q11619891|lt=Hakusan-hime-no-Kami|1=Kukurihime}}, and a shrine was built and worshiped on Mt. Hakusan summit. At this time, the nearby {{ill|lt=Hossō|Q349849|WD=Q349849|1=East Asian Yogācāra}} temple Shinpuku-ji was renamed {{ill|lt=Kiburi-ji|Q11523695|WD=Q11523695|1=Kiburi-ji}}. In {{ill|lt=1530|Q6429|WD=Q6429|1=1530}} ({{ill|lt=Kyōroku|Q1193418|WD=Q1193418|1=Kyōroku}} 3), floods damaged buildings at the foot, and in {{ill|lt=1560|Q6613|WD=Q6613|1=1560}} ({{ill|lt=Eiroku|Q1143341|WD=Q1143341|1=Eiroku}} 3), it was burned by {{ill|lt=Oda Nobunaga|Q171411|WD=Q171411|1=Oda Nobunaga}}, and the temple complex was lost. It was later restored with aid such as {{ill|lt=vermilion-seal letters|Q11093673|WD=Q11093673|1=shuinjō}} issued by {{ill|lt=Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Q187550|WD=Q187550|1=Toyotomi Hideyoshi}} and {{ill|lt=Tokugawa Ieyasu|Q171977|WD=Q171977|1=Tokugawa Ieyasu}}, and in {{ill|lt=1677|Q7631|WD=Q7631|1=1677}} ({{ill|lt=Enpō|Q1145735|WD=Q1145735|1=Enpō}} 5) new shrine buildings were constructed with support of the Toda clan of {{ill|lt=Ōgaki Domain|Q839430|WD=Q839430|1=Ōgaki Domain}}. Thereafter the Toda clan of Ōgaki Domain protected the shrine through repairs. It had long stood on the summit of Hakusan (317 m), but because Hakusan area had high-quality {{ill|lt=limestone|Q23757|WD=Q23757|1=Limestone}}, {{ill|lt=lime|Q250423|WD=Q250423|1=Lime (material)}} mining began around the area from the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Meiji period|1=Meiji era|WD=Q3125096}}. In {{ill|lt=1968|Q2644|WD=Q2644|1=1968}} (Shōwa 43), Hakusan was to be cut for mining, and Kiburi Shrine moved to its current site at the foot of the mountain. In {{ill|lt=1999|Q2091|WD=Q2091|1=1999}} (Heisei 11), surrounding areas were developed as "Kiburi Fureai Park." === Cultural properties === *Town-designated cultural properties **Chōzu stone (Important Cultural Property, craft){{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://heritage-ono.jp/shiteibunkazai.html |title=List of designated cultural properties |publisher=Ōno Town |accessdate=2013-04-20 |archivedate=2013-05-01 |archiveurl=https://archive.fo/tbePM}} **Kiburi-bayashi (intangible folk cultural property) **Fusulina fossils (natural monument) === Location === *Inatomi Aza Hakusan 2586, Ōno-chō, Ibi District, Gifu Prefecture === Transportation === *About 3.5 km from {{ill|lt=Motosu Station|Q6918526|WD=Q6918526|1=Motosu Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Tarumi Railway|Q11543718|WD=Q11543718|1=Tarumi Railway}}. *About 3.5 km from {{ill|lt=Kochibora Station|Q6424878|WD=Q6424878|1=Kochibora Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Tarumi Railway|Q11543718|WD=Q11543718|1=Tarumi Railway}}. *About 5 km from {{ill|lt=Ōno Bus Center|Q11439494|WD=Q11439494|1=Ōno Bus Center}} via {{ill|lt=Meihan Kintetsu Bus|Q11415818|WD=Q11415818|1=Meihan Kintetsu Bus}} / {{ill|lt=Gifu Bus|Q5560101|WD=Q5560101|lt=Gifu Bus|1=Gifu Bus}}. === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === External links === {{commonscat-inline|Kiburi-jinja}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:きふりしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kiburi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kiburi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mino Province]] [[Category:岐阜県の神社]] [[Category:大野町の歴史]] [[Category:美濃国の式内社]] [[Category:岐阜県の旧郷社]] [[Category:岐阜県内の市町村指定有形文化財]] [[Category:区市町村指定無形民俗文化財]] [[Category:区市町村指定天然記念物]] [[Category:大垣藩]] [[Category:大野町の建築物]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:qqq]]

In {{ill|lt=1999|Q2091|WD=Q2091|1=1999}} (Heisei 11), surrounding areas were developed as "Kiburi Fureai Park." === Cultural properties === *Town-designated cultural properties **Chōzu stone (Important Cultural Property, craft){{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://heritage-ono.jp/shiteibunkazai.html |title=List of designated cultural properties |publisher=Ōno Town |accessdate=2013-04-20 |archivedate=2013-05-01 |archiveurl=https://archive.fo/tbePM}} **Kiburi-bayashi (intangible folk cultural property) **Fusulina fossils (natural monument) === Location === *Inatomi Aza Hakusan 2586, Ōno-chō, Ibi District, Gifu Prefecture === Transportation === *About 3.5 km from {{ill|lt=Motosu Station|Q6918526|WD=Q6918526|1=Motosu Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Tarumi Railway|Q11543718|WD=Q11543718|1=Tarumi Railway}}. *About 3.5 km from {{ill|lt=Kochibora Station|Q6424878|WD=Q6424878|1=Kochibora Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Tarumi Railway|Q11543718|WD=Q11543718|1=Tarumi Railway}}. *About 5 km from {{ill|lt=Ōno Bus Center|Q11439494|WD=Q11439494|1=Ōno Bus Center}} via {{ill|lt=Meihan Kintetsu Bus|Q11415818|WD=Q11415818|1=Meihan Kintetsu Bus}} / {{ill|lt=Gifu Bus|Q5560101|WD=Q5560101|lt=Gifu Bus|1=Gifu Bus}}. === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === External links === {{commonscat-inline|Kiburi-jinja}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:きふりしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kiburi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kiburi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mino Province]] [[Category:岐阜県の神社]] [[Category:大野町の歴史]] [[Category:美濃国の式内社]] [[Category:岐阜県の旧郷社]] [[Category:岐阜県内の市町村指定有形文化財]] [[Category:区市町村指定無形民俗文化財]] [[Category:区市町村指定天然記念物]] [[Category:大垣藩]] [[Category:大野町の建築物]] [[Category:pages]]

link|Q11523697}} == Japanese content == {{神社 | 名称=Kiburi Shrine | 画像=[[Image:Kiburi Jinjya01.jpg|250px]]
Torii and haiden | 所在地={{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277|1=Gifu Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Ibi District|Q1204309|WD=Q1204309|1=Ibi District, Gifu}}{{ill|lt=Ōno|Q1203308|WD=Q1203308|1=Ōno, Gifu}}Inatomi Aza Hakusan 2586 | 位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | 祭神={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Izanagi-no-Mikoto|1=Izanagi|WD=Q813858}}、{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Izanami-no-Mikoto|1=Izanami|WD=Q682306}}、{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kikuri-hime-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}、{{ill|Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q276944|1=Ōkuninushi}}、Hakusan Hosaō | 創建=Traditionally {{ill|lt=Jinki|Q1373210|WD=Q1373210|1=Jinki (era)}} 2 ({{ill|lt=725|Q23552|WD=Q23552|1=725}}) | 社格={{ill|lt=small Shikinaisha|Q134917287|WD=Q134917287|1=Shikinai Shosha}}, former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} | 例祭=Grand annual festival (first Sunday of April) | 本殿= | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Gifu|label=Kiburi Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Kiburi Shrine''' (きぶりじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Ōno|Q1203308|WD=Q1203308|1=Ōno, Gifu}}, {{ill|lt=Ibi District|Q1204309|WD=Q1204309|1=Ibi District, Gifu}}, {{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277|1=Gifu Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|lt=Shikinaisha|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} ({{ill|lt=Mino Province|Q864156|WD=Q864156|lt=Mino no Kuni|1=Mino Province}} {{ill|lt=Ōno District (Mino)|Q11439757|WD=Q11439757|lt=Ōno District|1=Ōno district}} Kiburi Shrine, Shōnii ranked Shikinaisha Kiburi Ōkami), and its former rank under the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. ===Enshrined deities=== *{{ill|lt=Izanagi|Q813858|WD=Q813858|lt=Izanagi-no-Mikoto|1=Izanagi}} *{{ill|lt=Izanami|Q682306|WD=Q682306|lt=Izanami-no-Mikoto|1=Izanami}} *{{ill|lt=Kikurihime-no-Kami|Q11619891|WD=Q11619891|lt=Kikuri-hime-no-Mikoto|1=Kukurihime}} *{{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōkuninushi}} *Hakusan Hosaō ===History=== According to shrine tradition, it was founded in {{ill|lt=725|Q23552|WD=Q23552|1=725}} ({{ill|lt=Jinki|Q1373210|WD=Q1373210|1=Jinki (era)}} 2). On lunar June 18 of Jinki 2, golden-colored snow reportedly fell on the summit of Mt. Hakusan in Ōno District of Mino Province. This phenomenon was interpreted by oracle as the descent of {{ill|lt=Kikurihime-no-Kami|Q11619891|WD=Q11619891|lt=Hakusan-hime-no-Kami|1=Kukurihime}}, and a shrine was built and worshiped on Mt. Hakusan summit. At this time, the nearby {{ill|lt=Hossō|Q349849|WD=Q349849|1=East Asian Yogācāra}} temple Shinpuku-ji was renamed {{ill|lt=Kiburi-ji|Q11523695|WD=Q11523695|1=Kiburi-ji}}. In {{ill|lt=1530|Q6429|WD=Q6429|1=1530}} ({{ill|lt=Kyōroku|Q1193418|WD=Q1193418|1=Kyōroku}} 3), floods damaged buildings at the foot, and in {{ill|lt=1560|Q6613|WD=Q6613|1=1560}} ({{ill|lt=Eiroku|Q1143341|WD=Q1143341|1=Eiroku}} 3), it was burned by {{ill|lt=Oda Nobunaga|Q171411|WD=Q171411|1=Oda Nobunaga}}, and the temple complex was lost. It was later restored with aid such as {{ill|lt=vermilion-seal letters|Q11093673|WD=Q11093673|1=shuinjō}} issued by {{ill|lt=Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Q187550|WD=Q187550|1=Toyotomi Hideyoshi}} and {{ill|lt=Tokugawa Ieyasu|Q171977|WD=Q171977|1=Tokugawa Ieyasu}}, and in {{ill|lt=1677|Q7631|WD=Q7631|1=1677}} ({{ill|lt=Enpō|Q1145735|WD=Q1145735|1=Enpō}} 5) new shrine buildings were constructed with support of the Toda clan of {{ill|lt=Ōgaki Domain|Q839430|WD=Q839430|1=Ōgaki Domain}}. Thereafter the Toda clan of Ōgaki Domain protected the shrine through repairs. It had long stood on the summit of Hakusan (317 m), but because Hakusan area had high-quality {{ill|lt=limestone|Q23757|WD=Q23757|1=Limestone}}, {{ill|lt=lime|Q250423|WD=Q250423|1=Lime (material)}} mining began around the area from the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Meiji period|1=Meiji era|WD=Q3125096}}. In {{ill|lt=1968|Q2644|WD=Q2644|1=1968}} (Shōwa 43), Hakusan was to be cut for mining, and Kiburi Shrine moved to its current site at the foot of the mountain. In {{ill|lt=1999|Q2091|WD=Q2091|1=1999}} (Heisei 11), surrounding areas were developed as "Kiburi Fureai Park." === Cultural properties === *Town-designated cultural properties **Chōzu stone (Important Cultural Property, craft){{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://heritage-ono.jp/shiteibunkazai.html |title=List of designated cultural properties |publisher=Ōno Town |accessdate=2013-04-20 |archivedate=2013-05-01 |archiveurl=https://archive.fo/tbePM}} **Kiburi-bayashi (intangible folk cultural property) **Fusulina fossils (natural monument) === Location === *Inatomi Aza Hakusan 2586, Ōno-chō, Ibi District, Gifu Prefecture === Transportation === *About 3.5 km from {{ill|lt=Motosu Station|Q6918526|WD=Q6918526|1=Motosu Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Tarumi Railway|Q11543718|WD=Q11543718|1=Tarumi Railway}}. *About 3.5 km from {{ill|lt=Kochibora Station|Q6424878|WD=Q6424878|1=Kochibora Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Tarumi Railway|Q11543718|WD=Q11543718|1=Tarumi Railway}}. *About 5 km from {{ill|lt=Ōno Bus Center|Q11439494|WD=Q11439494|1=Ōno Bus Center}} via {{ill|lt=Meihan Kintetsu Bus|Q11415818|WD=Q11415818|1=Meihan Kintetsu Bus}} / {{ill|lt=Gifu Bus|Q5560101|WD=Q5560101|lt=Gifu Bus|1=Gifu Bus}}. === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === External links === {{commonscat-inline|Kiburi-jinja}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kiburi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kiburi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mino Province]] [[Category:岐阜県の神社]] [[Category:大野町の歴史]] [[Category:美濃国の式内社]] [[Category:岐阜県の旧郷社]] [[Category:岐阜県内の市町村指定有形文化財]] [[Category:区市町村指定無形民俗文化財]] [[Category:区市町村指定天然記念物]] [[Category:大垣藩]] [[Category:大野町の建築物]] [[Category:pages]]

It is a {{ill|lt=Shikinaisha|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} ({{ill|lt=Mino Province|Q864156|WD=Q864156|lt=Mino no Kuni|1=Mino Province}} {{ill|lt=Ōno District (Mino)|Q11439757|WD=Q11439757|lt=Ōno District|1=Ōno district}} Kiburi Shrine, Shōnii ranked Shikinaisha Kiburi Ōkami), and its former rank under the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. ===Enshrined deities=== *{{ill|lt=Izanagi|Q813858|WD=Q813858|lt=Izanagi-no-Mikoto|1=Izanagi}} *{{ill|lt=Izanami|Q682306|WD=Q682306|lt=Izanami-no-Mikoto|1=Izanami}} *{{ill|lt=Kikurihime-no-Kami|Q11619891|WD=Q11619891|lt=Kikuri-hime-no-Mikoto|1=Kukurihime}} *{{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōkuninushi}} *Hakusan Hosaō ===History=== According to shrine tradition, it was founded in {{ill|lt=725|Q23552|WD=Q23552|1=725}} ({{ill|lt=Jinki|Q1373210|WD=Q1373210|1=Jinki (era)}} 2). On lunar June 18 of Jinki 2, golden-colored snow reportedly fell on the summit of Mt. Hakusan in Ōno District of Mino Province. This phenomenon was interpreted by oracle as the descent of {{ill|lt=Kikurihime-no-Kami|Q11619891|WD=Q11619891|lt=Hakusan-hime-no-Kami|1=Kukurihime}}, and a shrine was built and worshiped on Mt. Hakusan summit. At this time, the nearby {{ill|lt=Hossō|Q349849|WD=Q349849|1=East Asian Yogācāra}} temple Shinpuku-ji was renamed {{ill|lt=Kiburi-ji|Q11523695|WD=Q11523695|1=Kiburi-ji}}. In {{ill|lt=1530|Q6429|WD=Q6429|1=1530}} ({{ill|lt=Kyōroku|Q1193418|WD=Q1193418|1=Kyōroku}} 3), floods damaged buildings at the foot, and in {{ill|lt=1560|Q6613|WD=Q6613|1=1560}} ({{ill|lt=Eiroku|Q1143341|WD=Q1143341|1=Eiroku}} 3), it was burned by {{ill|lt=Oda Nobunaga|Q171411|WD=Q171411|1=Oda Nobunaga}}, and the temple complex was lost. It was later restored with aid such as {{ill|lt=vermilion-seal letters|Q11093673|WD=Q11093673|1=shuinjō}} issued by {{ill|lt=Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Q187550|WD=Q187550|1=Toyotomi Hideyoshi}} and {{ill|lt=Tokugawa Ieyasu|Q171977|WD=Q171977|1=Tokugawa Ieyasu}}, and in {{ill|lt=1677|Q7631|WD=Q7631|1=1677}} ({{ill|lt=Enpō|Q1145735|WD=Q1145735|1=Enpō}} 5) new shrine buildings were constructed with support of the Toda clan of {{ill|lt=Ōgaki Domain|Q839430|WD=Q839430|1=Ōgaki Domain}}. Thereafter the Toda clan of Ōgaki Domain protected the shrine through repairs. It had long stood on the summit of Hakusan (317 m), but because Hakusan area had high-quality {{ill|lt=limestone|Q23757|WD=Q23757|1=Limestone}}, {{ill|lt=lime|Q250423|WD=Q250423|1=Lime (material)}} mining began around the area from the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Meiji period|1=Meiji era|WD=Q3125096}}. In {{ill|lt=1968|Q2644|WD=Q2644|1=1968}} (Shōwa 43), Hakusan was to be cut for mining, and Kiburi Shrine moved to its current site at the foot of the mountain. In {{ill|lt=1999|Q2091|WD=Q2091|1=1999}} (Heisei 11), surrounding areas were developed as "Kiburi Fureai Park." === Cultural properties === *Town-designated cultural properties **Chōzu stone (Important Cultural Property, craft){{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://heritage-ono.jp/shiteibunkazai.html |title=List of designated cultural properties |publisher=Ōno Town |accessdate=2013-04-20 |archivedate=2013-05-01 |archiveurl=https://archive.fo/tbePM}} **Kiburi-bayashi (intangible folk cultural property) **Fusulina fossils (natural monument) === Location === *Inatomi Aza Hakusan 2586, Ōno-chō, Ibi District, Gifu Prefecture === Transportation === *About 3.5 km from {{ill|lt=Motosu Station|Q6918526|WD=Q6918526|1=Motosu Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Tarumi Railway|Q11543718|WD=Q11543718|1=Tarumi Railway}}. *About 3.5 km from {{ill|lt=Kochibora Station|Q6424878|WD=Q6424878|1=Kochibora Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Tarumi Railway|Q11543718|WD=Q11543718|1=Tarumi Railway}}. *About 5 km from {{ill|lt=Ōno Bus Center|Q11439494|WD=Q11439494|1=Ōno Bus Center}} via {{ill|lt=Meihan Kintetsu Bus|Q11415818|WD=Q11415818|1=Meihan Kintetsu Bus}} / {{ill|lt=Gifu Bus|Q5560101|WD=Q5560101|lt=Gifu Bus|1=Gifu Bus}}. === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === External links === {{commonscat-inline|Kiburi-jinja}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kiburi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kiburi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mino Province]] [[Category:岐阜県の神社]] [[Category:大野町の歴史]] [[Category:美濃国の式内社]] [[Category:岐阜県の旧郷社]] [[Category:岐阜県内の市町村指定有形文化財]] [[Category:区市町村指定無形民俗文化財]] [[Category:Ward and municipal designated natural monuments]] [[Category:大垣藩]] [[Category:大野町の建築物]] [[Category:pages]]

link|Q11523697}} == Japanese content == {{神社 | 名称=Kiburi Shrine | 画像=[[Image:Kiburi Jinjya01.jpg|250px]]
Torii and haiden | 所在地={{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277|1=Gifu Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Ibi District|Q1204309|WD=Q1204309|1=Ibi District, Gifu}}{{ill|lt=Ōno|Q1203308|WD=Q1203308|1=Ōno, Gifu}}Inatomi Aza Hakusan 2586 | 位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | 祭神={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Izanagi-no-Mikoto|1=Izanagi|WD=Q813858}}、{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Izanami-no-Mikoto|1=Izanami|WD=Q682306}}、{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kikuri-hime-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}、{{ill|Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q276944|1=Ōkuninushi}}、Hakusan Hosaō | 創建=Traditionally {{ill|lt=Jinki|Q1373210|WD=Q1373210|1=Jinki (era)}} 2 ({{ill|lt=725|Q23552|WD=Q23552|1=725}}) | 社格={{ill|lt=small Shikinaisha|Q134917287|WD=Q134917287|1=Shikinai Shosha}}, former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} | 例祭=Grand annual festival (first Sunday of April) | 本殿= | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Gifu|label=Kiburi Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Kiburi Shrine''' (きぶりじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Ōno|Q1203308|WD=Q1203308|1=Ōno, Gifu}}, {{ill|lt=Ibi District|Q1204309|WD=Q1204309|1=Ibi District, Gifu}}, {{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277|1=Gifu Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|lt=Shikinaisha|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} ({{ill|lt=Mino Province|Q864156|WD=Q864156|lt=Mino no Kuni|1=Mino Province}} {{ill|lt=Ōno District (Mino)|Q11439757|WD=Q11439757|lt=Ōno District|1=Ōno district}} Kiburi Shrine, Shōnii ranked Shikinaisha Kiburi Ōkami), and its former rank under the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. ===Enshrined deities=== *{{ill|lt=Izanagi|Q813858|WD=Q813858|lt=Izanagi-no-Mikoto|1=Izanagi}} *{{ill|lt=Izanami|Q682306|WD=Q682306|lt=Izanami-no-Mikoto|1=Izanami}} *{{ill|lt=Kikurihime-no-Kami|Q11619891|WD=Q11619891|lt=Kikuri-hime-no-Mikoto|1=Kukurihime}} *{{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōkuninushi}} *Hakusan Hosaō ===History=== According to shrine tradition, it was founded in {{ill|lt=725|Q23552|WD=Q23552|1=725}} ({{ill|lt=Jinki|Q1373210|WD=Q1373210|1=Jinki (era)}} 2). On lunar June 18 of Jinki 2, golden-colored snow reportedly fell on the summit of Mt. Hakusan in Ōno District of Mino Province. This phenomenon was interpreted by oracle as the descent of {{ill|lt=Kikurihime-no-Kami|Q11619891|WD=Q11619891|lt=Hakusan-hime-no-Kami|1=Kukurihime}}, and a shrine was built and worshiped on Mt. Hakusan summit. At this time, the nearby {{ill|lt=Hossō|Q349849|WD=Q349849|1=East Asian Yogācāra}} temple Shinpuku-ji was renamed {{ill|lt=Kiburi-ji|Q11523695|WD=Q11523695|1=Kiburi-ji}}. In {{ill|lt=1530|Q6429|WD=Q6429|1=1530}} ({{ill|lt=Kyōroku|Q1193418|WD=Q1193418|1=Kyōroku}} 3), floods damaged buildings at the foot, and in {{ill|lt=1560|Q6613|WD=Q6613|1=1560}} ({{ill|lt=Eiroku|Q1143341|WD=Q1143341|1=Eiroku}} 3), it was burned by {{ill|lt=Oda Nobunaga|Q171411|WD=Q171411|1=Oda Nobunaga}}, and the temple complex was lost. It was later restored with aid such as {{ill|lt=vermilion-seal letters|Q11093673|WD=Q11093673|1=shuinjō}} issued by {{ill|lt=Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Q187550|WD=Q187550|1=Toyotomi Hideyoshi}} and {{ill|lt=Tokugawa Ieyasu|Q171977|WD=Q171977|1=Tokugawa Ieyasu}}, and in {{ill|lt=1677|Q7631|WD=Q7631|1=1677}} ({{ill|lt=Enpō|Q1145735|WD=Q1145735|1=Enpō}} 5) new shrine buildings were constructed with support of the Toda clan of {{ill|lt=Ōgaki Domain|Q839430|WD=Q839430|1=Ōgaki Domain}}. Thereafter the Toda clan of Ōgaki Domain protected the shrine through repairs. It had long stood on the summit of Hakusan (317 m), but because Hakusan area had high-quality {{ill|lt=limestone|Q23757|WD=Q23757|1=Limestone}}, {{ill|lt=lime|Q250423|WD=Q250423|1=Lime (material)}} mining began around the area from the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Meiji period|1=Meiji era|WD=Q3125096}}. In {{ill|lt=1968|Q2644|WD=Q2644|1=1968}} (Shōwa 43), Hakusan was to be cut for mining, and Kiburi Shrine moved to its current site at the foot of the mountain. In {{ill|lt=1999|Q2091|WD=Q2091|1=1999}} (Heisei 11), surrounding areas were developed as "Kiburi Fureai Park." === Cultural properties === *Town-designated cultural properties **Chōzu stone (Important Cultural Property, craft){{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://heritage-ono.jp/shiteibunkazai.html |title=List of designated cultural properties |publisher=Ōno Town |accessdate=2013-04-20 |archivedate=2013-05-01 |archiveurl=https://archive.fo/tbePM}} **Kiburi-bayashi (intangible folk cultural property) **Fusulina fossils (natural monument) === Location === *Inatomi Aza Hakusan 2586, Ōno-chō, Ibi District, Gifu Prefecture === Transportation === *About 3.5 km from {{ill|lt=Motosu Station|Q6918526|WD=Q6918526|1=Motosu Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Tarumi Railway|Q11543718|WD=Q11543718|1=Tarumi Railway}}. *About 3.5 km from {{ill|lt=Kochibora Station|Q6424878|WD=Q6424878|1=Kochibora Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Tarumi Railway|Q11543718|WD=Q11543718|1=Tarumi Railway}}. *About 5 km from {{ill|lt=Ōno Bus Center|Q11439494|WD=Q11439494|1=Ōno Bus Center}} via {{ill|lt=Meihan Kintetsu Bus|Q11415818|WD=Q11415818|1=Meihan Kintetsu Bus}} / {{ill|lt=Gifu Bus|Q5560101|WD=Q5560101|lt=Gifu Bus|1=Gifu Bus}}. === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === External links === {{commonscat-inline|Kiburi-jinja}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kiburi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kiburi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mino Province]] [[Category:岐阜県の神社]] [[Category:大野町の歴史]] [[Category:美濃国の式内社]] [[Category:岐阜県の旧郷社]] [[Category:岐阜県内の市町村指定有形文化財]] [[Category:Municipality Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Properties]] [[Category:Ward and municipal designated natural monuments]] [[Category:Ōgaki Domain]] [[Category:大野町の建築物]] [[Category:pages]]

In {{ill|lt=1968|Q2644|WD=Q2644|1=1968}} (Shōwa 43), Hakusan was to be cut for mining, and Kiburi Shrine moved to its current site at the foot of the mountain. In {{ill|lt=1999|Q2091|WD=Q2091|1=1999}} (Heisei 11), surrounding areas were developed as "Kiburi Fureai Park." === Cultural properties === *Town-designated cultural properties **Chōzu stone (Important Cultural Property, craft){{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://heritage-ono.jp/shiteibunkazai.html |title=List of designated cultural properties |publisher=Ōno Town |accessdate=2013-04-20 |archivedate=2013-05-01 |archiveurl=https://archive.fo/tbePM}} **Kiburi-bayashi (intangible folk cultural property) **Fusulina fossils (natural monument) === Location === *Inatomi Aza Hakusan 2586, Ōno-chō, Ibi District, Gifu Prefecture === Transportation === *About 3.5 km from {{ill|lt=Motosu Station|Q6918526|WD=Q6918526|1=Motosu Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Tarumi Railway|Q11543718|WD=Q11543718|1=Tarumi Railway}}. *About 3.5 km from {{ill|lt=Kochibora Station|Q6424878|WD=Q6424878|1=Kochibora Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Tarumi Railway|Q11543718|WD=Q11543718|1=Tarumi Railway}}. *About 5 km from {{ill|lt=Ōno Bus Center|Q11439494|WD=Q11439494|1=Ōno Bus Center}} via {{ill|lt=Meihan Kintetsu Bus|Q11415818|WD=Q11415818|1=Meihan Kintetsu Bus}} / {{ill|lt=Gifu Bus|Q5560101|WD=Q5560101|lt=Gifu Bus|1=Gifu Bus}}. === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === External links === {{commonscat-inline|Kiburi-jinja}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kiburi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kiburi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mino Province]] [[Category:岐阜県の神社]] [[Category:大野町の歴史]] [[Category:美濃国の式内社]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:岐阜県内の市町村指定有形文化財]] [[Category:Municipality Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Properties]] [[Category:Ward and municipal designated natural monuments]] [[Category:Ōgaki Domain]] [[Category:大野町の建築物]] [[Category:pages]]

link|Q11523697}} == Japanese content == {{神社 | 名称=Kiburi Shrine | 画像=[[Image:Kiburi Jinjya01.jpg|250px]]
Torii and haiden | 所在地={{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277|1=Gifu Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Ibi District|Q1204309|WD=Q1204309|1=Ibi District, Gifu}}{{ill|lt=Ōno|Q1203308|WD=Q1203308|1=Ōno, Gifu}}Inatomi Aza Hakusan 2586 | 位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | 祭神={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Izanagi-no-Mikoto|1=Izanagi|WD=Q813858}}、{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Izanami-no-Mikoto|1=Izanami|WD=Q682306}}、{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kikuri-hime-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}、{{ill|Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q276944|1=Ōkuninushi}}、Hakusan Hosaō | 創建=Traditionally {{ill|lt=Jinki|Q1373210|WD=Q1373210|1=Jinki (era)}} 2 ({{ill|lt=725|Q23552|WD=Q23552|1=725}}) | 社格={{ill|lt=small Shikinaisha|Q134917287|WD=Q134917287|1=Shikinai Shosha}}, former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} | 例祭=Grand annual festival (first Sunday of April) | 本殿= | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Gifu|label=Kiburi Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Kiburi Shrine''' (きぶりじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Ōno|Q1203308|WD=Q1203308|1=Ōno, Gifu}}, {{ill|lt=Ibi District|Q1204309|WD=Q1204309|1=Ibi District, Gifu}}, {{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277|1=Gifu Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|lt=Shikinaisha|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} ({{ill|lt=Mino Province|Q864156|WD=Q864156|lt=Mino no Kuni|1=Mino Province}} {{ill|lt=Ōno District (Mino)|Q11439757|WD=Q11439757|lt=Ōno District|1=Ōno district}} Kiburi Shrine, Shōnii ranked Shikinaisha Kiburi Ōkami), and its former rank under the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. ===Enshrined deities=== *{{ill|lt=Izanagi|Q813858|WD=Q813858|lt=Izanagi-no-Mikoto|1=Izanagi}} *{{ill|lt=Izanami|Q682306|WD=Q682306|lt=Izanami-no-Mikoto|1=Izanami}} *{{ill|lt=Kikurihime-no-Kami|Q11619891|WD=Q11619891|lt=Kikuri-hime-no-Mikoto|1=Kukurihime}} *{{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōkuninushi}} *Hakusan Hosaō ===History=== According to shrine tradition, it was founded in {{ill|lt=725|Q23552|WD=Q23552|1=725}} ({{ill|lt=Jinki|Q1373210|WD=Q1373210|1=Jinki (era)}} 2). On lunar June 18 of Jinki 2, golden-colored snow reportedly fell on the summit of Mt. Hakusan in Ōno District of Mino Province. This phenomenon was interpreted by oracle as the descent of {{ill|lt=Kikurihime-no-Kami|Q11619891|WD=Q11619891|lt=Hakusan-hime-no-Kami|1=Kukurihime}}, and a shrine was built and worshiped on Mt. Hakusan summit. At this time, the nearby {{ill|lt=Hossō|Q349849|WD=Q349849|1=East Asian Yogācāra}} temple Shinpuku-ji was renamed {{ill|lt=Kiburi-ji|Q11523695|WD=Q11523695|1=Kiburi-ji}}. In {{ill|lt=1530|Q6429|WD=Q6429|1=1530}} ({{ill|lt=Kyōroku|Q1193418|WD=Q1193418|1=Kyōroku}} 3), floods damaged buildings at the foot, and in {{ill|lt=1560|Q6613|WD=Q6613|1=1560}} ({{ill|lt=Eiroku|Q1143341|WD=Q1143341|1=Eiroku}} 3), it was burned by {{ill|lt=Oda Nobunaga|Q171411|WD=Q171411|1=Oda Nobunaga}}, and the temple complex was lost. It was later restored with aid such as {{ill|lt=vermilion-seal letters|Q11093673|WD=Q11093673|1=shuinjō}} issued by {{ill|lt=Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Q187550|WD=Q187550|1=Toyotomi Hideyoshi}} and {{ill|lt=Tokugawa Ieyasu|Q171977|WD=Q171977|1=Tokugawa Ieyasu}}, and in {{ill|lt=1677|Q7631|WD=Q7631|1=1677}} ({{ill|lt=Enpō|Q1145735|WD=Q1145735|1=Enpō}} 5) new shrine buildings were constructed with support of the Toda clan of {{ill|lt=Ōgaki Domain|Q839430|WD=Q839430|1=Ōgaki Domain}}. Thereafter the Toda clan of Ōgaki Domain protected the shrine through repairs. It had long stood on the summit of Hakusan (317 m), but because Hakusan area had high-quality {{ill|lt=limestone|Q23757|WD=Q23757|1=Limestone}}, {{ill|lt=lime|Q250423|WD=Q250423|1=Lime (material)}} mining began around the area from the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Meiji period|1=Meiji era|WD=Q3125096}}. In {{ill|lt=1968|Q2644|WD=Q2644|1=1968}} (Shōwa 43), Hakusan was to be cut for mining, and Kiburi Shrine moved to its current site at the foot of the mountain. In {{ill|lt=1999|Q2091|WD=Q2091|1=1999}} (Heisei 11), surrounding areas were developed as "Kiburi Fureai Park." === Cultural properties === *Town-designated cultural properties **Chōzu stone (Important Cultural Property, craft){{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://heritage-ono.jp/shiteibunkazai.html |title=List of designated cultural properties |publisher=Ōno Town |accessdate=2013-04-20 |archivedate=2013-05-01 |archiveurl=https://archive.fo/tbePM}} **Kiburi-bayashi (intangible folk cultural property) **Fusulina fossils (natural monument) === Location === *Inatomi Aza Hakusan 2586, Ōno-chō, Ibi District, Gifu Prefecture === Transportation === *About 3.5 km from {{ill|lt=Motosu Station|Q6918526|WD=Q6918526|1=Motosu Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Tarumi Railway|Q11543718|WD=Q11543718|1=Tarumi Railway}}. *About 3.5 km from {{ill|lt=Kochibora Station|Q6424878|WD=Q6424878|1=Kochibora Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Tarumi Railway|Q11543718|WD=Q11543718|1=Tarumi Railway}}. *About 5 km from {{ill|lt=Ōno Bus Center|Q11439494|WD=Q11439494|1=Ōno Bus Center}} via {{ill|lt=Meihan Kintetsu Bus|Q11415818|WD=Q11415818|1=Meihan Kintetsu Bus}} / {{ill|lt=Gifu Bus|Q5560101|WD=Q5560101|lt=Gifu Bus|1=Gifu Bus}}. === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === External links === {{commonscat-inline|Kiburi-jinja}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kiburi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kiburi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mino Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:大野町の歴史]] [[Category:美濃国の式内社]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:岐阜県内の市町村指定有形文化財]] [[Category:Municipality Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Properties]] [[Category:Ward and municipal designated natural monuments]] [[Category:Ōgaki Domain]] [[Category:大野町の建築物]] [[Category:pages]]

It is a {{ill|lt=Shikinaisha|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} ({{ill|lt=Mino Province|Q864156|WD=Q864156|lt=Mino no Kuni|1=Mino Province}} {{ill|lt=Ōno District (Mino)|Q11439757|WD=Q11439757|lt=Ōno District|1=Ōno district}} Kiburi Shrine, Shōnii ranked Shikinaisha Kiburi Ōkami), and its former rank under the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. ===Enshrined deities=== *{{ill|lt=Izanagi|Q813858|WD=Q813858|lt=Izanagi-no-Mikoto|1=Izanagi}} *{{ill|lt=Izanami|Q682306|WD=Q682306|lt=Izanami-no-Mikoto|1=Izanami}} *{{ill|lt=Kikurihime-no-Kami|Q11619891|WD=Q11619891|lt=Kikuri-hime-no-Mikoto|1=Kukurihime}} *{{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōkuninushi}} *Hakusan Hosaō ===History=== According to shrine tradition, it was founded in {{ill|lt=725|Q23552|WD=Q23552|1=725}} ({{ill|lt=Jinki|Q1373210|WD=Q1373210|1=Jinki (era)}} 2). On lunar June 18 of Jinki 2, golden-colored snow reportedly fell on the summit of Mt. Hakusan in Ōno District of Mino Province. This phenomenon was interpreted by oracle as the descent of {{ill|lt=Kikurihime-no-Kami|Q11619891|WD=Q11619891|lt=Hakusan-hime-no-Kami|1=Kukurihime}}, and a shrine was built and worshiped on Mt. Hakusan summit. At this time, the nearby {{ill|lt=Hossō|Q349849|WD=Q349849|1=East Asian Yogācāra}} temple Shinpuku-ji was renamed {{ill|lt=Kiburi-ji|Q11523695|WD=Q11523695|1=Kiburi-ji}}. In {{ill|lt=1530|Q6429|WD=Q6429|1=1530}} ({{ill|lt=Kyōroku|Q1193418|WD=Q1193418|1=Kyōroku}} 3), floods damaged buildings at the foot, and in {{ill|lt=1560|Q6613|WD=Q6613|1=1560}} ({{ill|lt=Eiroku|Q1143341|WD=Q1143341|1=Eiroku}} 3), it was burned by {{ill|lt=Oda Nobunaga|Q171411|WD=Q171411|1=Oda Nobunaga}}, and the temple complex was lost. It was later restored with aid such as {{ill|lt=vermilion-seal letters|Q11093673|WD=Q11093673|1=shuinjō}} issued by {{ill|lt=Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Q187550|WD=Q187550|1=Toyotomi Hideyoshi}} and {{ill|lt=Tokugawa Ieyasu|Q171977|WD=Q171977|1=Tokugawa Ieyasu}}, and in {{ill|lt=1677|Q7631|WD=Q7631|1=1677}} ({{ill|lt=Enpō|Q1145735|WD=Q1145735|1=Enpō}} 5) new shrine buildings were constructed with support of the Toda clan of {{ill|lt=Ōgaki Domain|Q839430|WD=Q839430|1=Ōgaki Domain}}. Thereafter the Toda clan of Ōgaki Domain protected the shrine through repairs. It had long stood on the summit of Hakusan (317 m), but because Hakusan area had high-quality {{ill|lt=limestone|Q23757|WD=Q23757|1=Limestone}}, {{ill|lt=lime|Q250423|WD=Q250423|1=Lime (material)}} mining began around the area from the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Meiji period|1=Meiji era|WD=Q3125096}}. In {{ill|lt=1968|Q2644|WD=Q2644|1=1968}} (Shōwa 43), Hakusan was to be cut for mining, and Kiburi Shrine moved to its current site at the foot of the mountain. In {{ill|lt=1999|Q2091|WD=Q2091|1=1999}} (Heisei 11), surrounding areas were developed as "Kiburi Fureai Park." === Cultural properties === *Town-designated cultural properties **Chōzu stone (Important Cultural Property, craft){{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://heritage-ono.jp/shiteibunkazai.html |title=List of designated cultural properties |publisher=Ōno Town |accessdate=2013-04-20 |archivedate=2013-05-01 |archiveurl=https://archive.fo/tbePM}} **Kiburi-bayashi (intangible folk cultural property) **Fusulina fossils (natural monument) === Location === *Inatomi Aza Hakusan 2586, Ōno-chō, Ibi District, Gifu Prefecture === Transportation === *About 3.5 km from {{ill|lt=Motosu Station|Q6918526|WD=Q6918526|1=Motosu Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Tarumi Railway|Q11543718|WD=Q11543718|1=Tarumi Railway}}. *About 3.5 km from {{ill|lt=Kochibora Station|Q6424878|WD=Q6424878|1=Kochibora Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Tarumi Railway|Q11543718|WD=Q11543718|1=Tarumi Railway}}. *About 5 km from {{ill|lt=Ōno Bus Center|Q11439494|WD=Q11439494|1=Ōno Bus Center}} via {{ill|lt=Meihan Kintetsu Bus|Q11415818|WD=Q11415818|1=Meihan Kintetsu Bus}} / {{ill|lt=Gifu Bus|Q5560101|WD=Q5560101|lt=Gifu Bus|1=Gifu Bus}}. === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === External links === {{commonscat-inline|Kiburi-jinja}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kiburi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kiburi Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mino Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:大野町の歴史]] [[Category:美濃国の式内社]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal Tangible Cultural Properties in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Municipality Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Properties]] [[Category:Ward and municipal designated natural monuments]] [[Category:Ōgaki Domain]] [[Category:大野町の建築物]] [[Category:pages]]

Torii and haiden | 所在地={{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277|1=Gifu Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Ibi District|Q1204309|WD=Q1204309|1=Ibi District, Gifu}}{{ill|lt=Ōno|Q1203308|WD=Q1203308|1=Ōno, Gifu}}Inatomi Aza Hakusan 2586 | 位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | 祭神={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Izanagi-no-Mikoto|1=Izanagi|WD=Q813858}}、{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Izanami-no-Mikoto|1=Izanami|WD=Q682306}}、{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kikuri-hime-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}、{{ill|Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q276944|1=Ōkuninushi}}、Hakusan Hosaō | 創建=Traditionally {{ill|lt=Jinki|Q1373210|WD=Q1373210|1=Jinki (era)}} 2 ({{ill|lt=725|Q23552|WD=Q23552|1=725}}) | 社格={{ill|lt=small Shikinaisha|Q134917287|WD=Q134917287|1=Shikinai Shosha}}, former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} | 例祭=Grand annual festival (first Sunday of April) | 本殿= | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Gifu|label=Kiburi Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Kiburi Shrine''' (きぶりじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Ōno|Q1203308|WD=Q1203308|1=Ōno, Gifu}}, {{ill|lt=Ibi District|Q1204309|WD=Q1204309|1=Ibi District, Gifu}}, {{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277|1=Gifu Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|lt=Shikinaisha|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} ({{ill|lt=Mino Province|Q864156|WD=Q864156|lt=Mino no Kuni|1=Mino Province}} {{ill|lt=Ōno District (Mino)|Q11439757|WD=Q11439757|lt=Ōno District|1=Ōno district}} Kiburi Shrine, Shōnii ranked Shikinaisha Kiburi Ōkami), and its former rank under the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. ===Enshrined deities=== *{{ill|lt=Izanagi|Q813858|WD=Q813858|lt=Izanagi-no-Mikoto|1=Izanagi}} *{{ill|lt=Izanami|Q682306|WD=Q682306|lt=Izanami-no-Mikoto|1=Izanami}} *{{ill|lt=Kikurihime-no-Kami|Q11619891|WD=Q11619891|lt=Kikuri-hime-no-Mikoto|1=Kukurihime}} *{{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|WD=Q276944|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōkuninushi}} *Hakusan Hosaō ===History=== According to shrine tradition, it was founded in {{ill|lt=725|Q23552|WD=Q23552|1=725}} ({{ill|lt=Jinki|Q1373210|WD=Q1373210|1=Jinki (era)}} 2). On lunar June 18 of Jinki 2, golden-colored snow reportedly fell on the summit of Mt. Hakusan in Ōno District of Mino Province. This phenomenon was interpreted by oracle as the descent of {{ill|lt=Kikurihime-no-Kami|Q11619891|WD=Q11619891|lt=Hakusan-hime-no-Kami|1=Kukurihime}}, and a shrine was built and worshiped on Mt. Hakusan summit. At this time, the nearby {{ill|lt=Hossō|Q349849|WD=Q349849|1=East Asian Yogācāra}} temple Shinpuku-ji was renamed {{ill|lt=Kiburi-ji|Q11523695|WD=Q11523695|1=Kiburi-ji}}. In {{ill|lt=1530|Q6429|WD=Q6429|1=1530}} ({{ill|lt=Kyōroku|Q1193418|WD=Q1193418|1=Kyōroku}} 3), floods damaged buildings at the foot, and in {{ill|lt=1560|Q6613|WD=Q6613|1=1560}} ({{ill|lt=Eiroku|Q1143341|WD=Q1143341|1=Eiroku}} 3), it was burned by {{ill|lt=Oda Nobunaga|Q171411|WD=Q171411|1=Oda Nobunaga}}, and the temple complex was lost. It was later restored with aid such as {{ill|lt=vermilion-seal letters|Q11093673|WD=Q11093673|1=shuinjō}} issued by {{ill|lt=Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Q187550|WD=Q187550|1=Toyotomi Hideyoshi}} and {{ill|lt=Tokugawa Ieyasu|Q171977|WD=Q171977|1=Tokugawa Ieyasu}}, and in {{ill|lt=1677|Q7631|WD=Q7631|1=1677}} ({{ill|lt=Enpō|Q1145735|WD=Q1145735|1=Enpō}} 5) new shrine buildings were constructed with support of the Toda clan of {{ill|lt=Ōgaki Domain|Q839430|WD=Q839430|1=Ōgaki Domain}}. Thereafter the Toda clan of Ōgaki Domain protected the shrine through repairs. It had long stood on the summit of Hakusan (317 m), but because Hakusan area had high-quality {{ill|lt=limestone|Q23757|WD=Q23757|1=Limestone}}, {{ill|lt=lime|Q250423|WD=Q250423|1=Lime (material)}} mining began around the area from the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Meiji period|1=Meiji era|WD=Q3125096}}. In {{ill|lt=1968|Q2644|WD=Q2644|1=1968}} (Shōwa 43), Hakusan was to be cut for mining, and Kiburi Shrine moved to its current site at the foot of the mountain. In {{ill|lt=1999|Q2091|WD=Q2091|1=1999}} (Heisei 11), surrounding areas were developed as "Kiburi Fureai Park." === Cultural properties === *Town-designated cultural properties **Chōzu stone (Important Cultural Property, craft){{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://heritage-ono.jp/shiteibunkazai.html |title=List of designated cultural properties |publisher=Ōno Town |accessdate=2013-04-20 |archivedate=2013-05-01 |archiveurl=https://archive.fo/tbePM}} **Kiburi-bayashi (intangible folk cultural property) **Fusulina fossils (natural monument) === Location === *Inatomi Aza Hakusan 2586, Ōno-chō, Ibi District, Gifu Prefecture === Transportation === *About 3.5 km from {{ill|lt=Motosu Station|Q6918526|WD=Q6918526|1=Motosu Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Tarumi Railway|Q11543718|WD=Q11543718|1=Tarumi Railway}}. *About 3.5 km from {{ill|lt=Kochibora Station|Q6424878|WD=Q6424878|1=Kochibora Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Tarumi Railway|Q11543718|WD=Q11543718|1=Tarumi Railway}}. *About 5 km from {{ill|lt=Ōno Bus Center|Q11439494|WD=Q11439494|1=Ōno Bus Center}} via {{ill|lt=Meihan Kintetsu Bus|Q11415818|WD=Q11415818|1=Meihan Kintetsu Bus}} / {{ill|lt=Gifu Bus|Q5560101|WD=Q5560101|lt=Gifu Bus|1=Gifu Bus}}. === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === External links === {{commonscat-inline|Kiburi-jinja}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kiburi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kiburi Shrine]][[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mino Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:大野町の歴史]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mino Province]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal Tangible Cultural Properties in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Municipality Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Properties]] [[Category:Ward and municipal designated natural monuments]] [[Category:Ōgaki Domain]] [[Category:大野町の建築物]]

link|Q11523697}} == Japanese content == {{神社 | 名称=Kiburi Shrine | 画像=[[Image:Kiburi Jinjya01.jpg|250px]]
Torii and haiden | 所在地={{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|qid=Q131277|1=Gifu Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Ibi District|Q1204309|qid=Q1204309|1=Ibi District, Gifu}}{{ill|lt=Ōno|Q1203308|qid=Q1203308|1=Ōno, Gifu}}Inatomi Aza Hakusan 2586 | 位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | 祭神={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Izanagi-no-Mikoto|1=Izanagi|qid=Q813858}}、{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Izanami-no-Mikoto|1=Izanami|qid=Q682306}}、{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kikuri-hime-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}、{{ill|Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|qid=Q276944|1=Ōkuninushi}}、Hakusan Hosaō | 創建=Traditionally {{ill|lt=Jinki|Q1373210|qid=Q1373210|1=Jinki (era)}} 2 ({{ill|lt=725|Q23552|qid=Q23552|1=725}}) | 社格={{ill|lt=small Shikinaisha|Q134917287|qid=Q134917287|1=Shikinai Shosha}}, former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|qid=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} | 例祭=Grand annual festival (first Sunday of April) | 本殿= | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Gifu|label=Kiburi Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Kiburi Shrine''' (きぶりじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Ōno|Q1203308|qid=Q1203308|1=Ōno, Gifu}}, {{ill|lt=Ibi District|Q1204309|qid=Q1204309|1=Ibi District, Gifu}}, {{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|qid=Q131277|1=Gifu Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|lt=Shikinaisha|Q11064932|qid=Q11064932|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} ({{ill|lt=Mino Province|Q864156|qid=Q864156|lt=Mino no Kuni|1=Mino Province}} {{ill|lt=Ōno District (Mino)|Q11439757|qid=Q11439757|lt=Ōno District|1=Ōno district}} Kiburi Shrine, Shōnii ranked Shikinaisha Kiburi Ōkami), and its former rank under the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|qid=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|qid=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. ===Enshrined deities=== *{{ill|lt=Izanagi|Q813858|qid=Q813858|lt=Izanagi-no-Mikoto|1=Izanagi}} *{{ill|lt=Izanami|Q682306|qid=Q682306|lt=Izanami-no-Mikoto|1=Izanami}} *{{ill|lt=Kikurihime-no-Kami|Q11619891|qid=Q11619891|lt=Kikuri-hime-no-Mikoto|1=Kikurihime}} *{{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|qid=Q276944|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōkuninushi}} *Hakusan Hosaō ===History=== According to shrine tradition, it was founded in {{ill|lt=725|Q23552|qid=Q23552|1=725}} ({{ill|lt=Jinki|Q1373210|qid=Q1373210|1=Jinki (era)}} 2). On lunar June 18 of Jinki 2, golden-colored snow reportedly fell on the summit of Mt. Hakusan in Ōno District of Mino Province. This phenomenon was interpreted by oracle as the descent of {{ill|lt=Kikurihime-no-Kami|Q11619891|qid=Q11619891|lt=Hakusan-hime-no-Kami|1=Kikurihime}}, and a shrine was built and worshiped on Mt. Hakusan summit. At this time, the nearby {{ill|lt=Hossō|Q349849|qid=Q349849|1=East Asian Yogācāra}} temple Shinpuku-ji was renamed {{ill|lt=Kiburi-ji|Q11523695|qid=Q11523695|1=Kiburi-ji}}. In {{ill|lt=1530|Q6429|qid=Q6429|1=1530}} ({{ill|lt=Kyōroku|Q1193418|qid=Q1193418|1=Kyōroku}} 3), floods damaged buildings at the foot, and in {{ill|lt=1560|Q6613|qid=Q6613|1=1560}} ({{ill|lt=Eiroku|Q1143341|qid=Q1143341|1=Eiroku}} 3), it was burned by {{ill|lt=Oda Nobunaga|Q171411|qid=Q171411|1=Oda Nobunaga}}, and the temple complex was lost. It was later restored with aid such as {{ill|lt=vermilion-seal letters|Q11093673|qid=Q11093673|1=shuinjō}} issued by {{ill|lt=Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Q187550|qid=Q187550|1=Toyotomi Hideyoshi}} and {{ill|lt=Tokugawa Ieyasu|Q171977|qid=Q171977|1=Tokugawa Ieyasu}}, and in {{ill|lt=1677|Q7631|qid=Q7631|1=1677}} ({{ill|lt=Enpō|Q1145735|qid=Q1145735|1=Enpō}} 5) new shrine buildings were constructed with support of the Toda clan of {{ill|lt=Ōgaki Domain|Q839430|qid=Q839430|1=Ōgaki Domain}}. Thereafter the Toda clan of Ōgaki Domain protected the shrine through repairs. It had long stood on the summit of Hakusan (317 m), but because Hakusan area had high-quality {{ill|lt=limestone|Q23757|qid=Q23757|1=Limestone}}, {{ill|lt=lime|Q250423|qid=Q250423|1=Lime (material)}} mining began around the area from the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Meiji period|1=Meiji era|qid=Q3125096}}. In {{ill|lt=1968|Q2644|qid=Q2644|1=1968}} (Shōwa 43), Hakusan was to be cut for mining, and Kiburi Shrine moved to its current site at the foot of the mountain. In {{ill|lt=1999|Q2091|qid=Q2091|1=1999}} (Heisei 11), surrounding areas were developed as "Kiburi Fureai Park." === Cultural properties === *Town-designated cultural properties **Chōzu stone (Important Cultural Property, craft){{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://heritage-ono.jp/shiteibunkazai.html |title=List of designated cultural properties |publisher=Ōno Town |accessdate=2013-04-20 |archivedate=2013-05-01 |archiveurl=https://archive.fo/tbePM}} **Kiburi-bayashi (intangible folk cultural property) **Fusulina fossils (natural monument) === Location === *Inatomi Aza Hakusan 2586, Ōno-chō, Ibi District, Gifu Prefecture === Transportation === *About 3.5 km from {{ill|lt=Motosu Station|Q6918526|qid=Q6918526|1=Motosu Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Tarumi Railway|Q11543718|qid=Q11543718|1=Tarumi Railway}}. *About 3.5 km from {{ill|lt=Kochibora Station|Q6424878|qid=Q6424878|1=Kochibora Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Tarumi Railway|Q11543718|qid=Q11543718|1=Tarumi Railway}}. *About 5 km from {{ill|lt=Ōno Bus Center|Q11439494|qid=Q11439494|1=Ōno Bus Center}} via {{ill|lt=Meihan Kintetsu Bus|Q11415818|qid=Q11415818|1=Meihan Kintetsu Bus}} / {{ill|lt=Gifu Bus|Q5560101|qid=Q5560101|lt=Gifu Bus|1=Gifu Bus}}. === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === External links === {{commonscat-inline|Kiburi-jinja}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kiburi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kiburi Shrine]][[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mino Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:大野町の歴史]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mino Province]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal Tangible Cultural Properties in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Municipality Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Properties]] [[Category:Ward and municipal designated natural monuments]] [[Category:Ōgaki Domain]] [[Category:大野町の建築物]]

On lunar June 18 of Jinki 2, golden-colored snow reportedly fell on the summit of Mt. Hakusan in Ōno District of Mino Province. This phenomenon was interpreted by oracle as the descent of {{ill|lt=Kikurihime-no-Kami|Q11619891|qid=Q11619891|lt=Hakusan-hime-no-Kami|1=Kikurihime}}, and a shrine was built and worshiped on Mt. Hakusan summit. At this time, the nearby {{ill|lt=Hossō|Q349849|qid=Q349849|1=East Asian Yogācāra}} temple Shinpuku-ji was renamed {{ill|lt=Kiburi-ji|Q11523695|qid=Q11523695|1=Kiburi-ji}}. In {{ill|lt=1530|Q6429|qid=Q6429|1=1530}} ({{ill|lt=Kyōroku|Q1193418|qid=Q1193418|1=Kyōroku}} 3), floods damaged buildings at the foot, and in {{ill|lt=1560|Q6613|qid=Q6613|1=1560}} ({{ill|lt=Eiroku|Q1143341|qid=Q1143341|1=Eiroku}} 3), it was burned by {{ill|lt=Oda Nobunaga|Q171411|qid=Q171411|1=Oda Nobunaga}}, and the temple complex was lost. It was later restored with aid such as {{ill|lt=vermilion-seal letters|Q11093673|qid=Q11093673|1=shuinjō}} issued by {{ill|lt=Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Q187550|qid=Q187550|1=Toyotomi Hideyoshi}} and {{ill|lt=Tokugawa Ieyasu|Q171977|qid=Q171977|1=Tokugawa Ieyasu}}, and in {{ill|lt=1677|Q7631|qid=Q7631|1=1677}} ({{ill|lt=Enpō|Q1145735|qid=Q1145735|1=Enpō}} 5) new shrine buildings were constructed with support of the Toda clan of {{ill|lt=Ōgaki Domain|Q839430|qid=Q839430|1=Ōgaki Domain}}. Thereafter the Toda clan of Ōgaki Domain protected the shrine through repairs. It had long stood on the summit of Hakusan (317 m), but because Hakusan area had high-quality {{ill|lt=limestone|Q23757|qid=Q23757|1=Limestone}}, {{ill|lt=lime|Q250423|qid=Q250423|1=Lime (material)}} mining began around the area from the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Meiji period|1=Meiji era|qid=Q3125096}}. In {{ill|lt=1968|Q2644|qid=Q2644|1=1968}} (Shōwa 43), Hakusan was to be cut for mining, and Kiburi Shrine moved to its current site at the foot of the mountain. In {{ill|lt=1999|Q2091|qid=Q2091|1=1999}} (Heisei 11), surrounding areas were developed as "Kiburi Fureai Park." === Cultural properties === *Town-designated cultural properties **Chōzu stone (Important Cultural Property, craft){{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://heritage-ono.jp/shiteibunkazai.html |title=List of designated cultural properties |publisher=Ōno Town |accessdate=2013-04-20 |archivedate=2013-05-01 |archiveurl=https://archive.fo/tbePM}} **Kiburi-bayashi (intangible folk cultural property) **Fusulina fossils (natural monument) === Location === *Inatomi Aza Hakusan 2586, Ōno-chō, Ibi District, Gifu Prefecture === Transportation === *About 3.5 km from {{ill|lt=Motosu Station|Q6918526|qid=Q6918526|1=Motosu Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Tarumi Railway|Q11543718|qid=Q11543718|1=Tarumi Railway}}. *About 3.5 km from {{ill|lt=Kochibora Station|Q6424878|qid=Q6424878|1=Kochibora Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Tarumi Railway|Q11543718|qid=Q11543718|1=Tarumi Railway}}. *About 5 km from {{ill|lt=Ōno Bus Center|Q11439494|qid=Q11439494|1=Ōno Bus Center}} via {{ill|lt=Meihan Kintetsu Bus|Q11415818|qid=Q11415818|1=Meihan Kintetsu Bus}} / {{ill|lt=Gifu Bus|Q5560101|qid=Q5560101|lt=Gifu Bus|1=Gifu Bus}}. === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === External links === {{commonscat-inline|Kiburi-jinja}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kiburi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kiburi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mino Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:大野町の歴史]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mino Province]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal Tangible Cultural Properties in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Municipality Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Properties]] [[Category:Ward and municipal designated natural monuments]] [[Category:Ōgaki Domain]] [[Category:大野町の建築物]]
